name: Caramel Surprise Cake
recipeInstructions: Freeze caramel candies until ready to use. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Generously butter and flour eight 1-cup ramekins and set on a cookie sheet. Chop chocolate into small pieces. Melt chocolate with butter in a double boiler over simmering water, removing from heat and stirring until melted and smooth. Cool. Beat eggs, egg yolks, and sugar together for about 6 minutes; the mixture will turn pale yellow and thicken. Fold in cooled chocolate mixture and vanilla extract. Sift flour over the chocolate and fold in. Pour batter into ramekins. Bake for about 7-8 minutes. Remove from oven and insert two frozen caramel candies into the center of half-baked cakes. Return to oven and bake another 7-8 minutes. Cool, on a wire rack, for about 10 minutes. Chop chocolate into pieces. Heat half the cream with sugar in a saucepan until boiling; remove from heat and add chocolate, stirring until it melts and is smooth. Add remaining cream and stir until smooth. Warm the jar of caramel sauce. Pour caramel sauce onto each dessert plate, making a pool in the bottom of each. Drizzle chocolate sauce in zigzag patterns over the caramel sauce and feather with a toothpick if desired. Loosen cakes with a sharp knife and invert onto sauces. Top with more chocolate sauce and serve.
